text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Two common types are unit tests and integration tests. Unit tests check small pieces of code, like a single function, while integration tests ensure different parts of a system work together correctly. Many developers use test-driven development, or TDD, where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps catch errors early and guides the design of the software. Overall, thorough testing gives developers confidence. They can make changes or add new features knowing that existing functionality still works. This saves time and reduces stress, especially in large projects.
